message:
feat(extensions): add PolicySnapshot for O(1) capability auth, HostcallRequestQueue with fast-path ring, and marshalling telemetry

PolicySnapshot (extensions.rs): Introduces a precomputed per-extension
capability decision table that replaces runtime evaluate_for() calls
with O(1) array lookups. PolicySnapshot::compile() builds the table
once from an ExtensionPolicy at dispatcher creation time. Each known
Capability gets an ordinal index() method for direct array access.
Unknown capabilities fall back to the original evaluate_for() path.

Also adds Capability::index() const fn returning ordinal 0..9 and
NUM_CAPABILITIES constant, plus hash_hostcall_envelope() to deduplicate
the identical JSON envelope hashing pattern between hostcall_params_hash
and hostcall_params_shape_hash.

Extends RuntimeHostcallTelemetryEvent with four marshalling telemetry
fields: marshalling_path (interned_opcode_arena_v1 or canonical_*),
marshalling_latency_us, marshalling_fallback_reason, and
marshalling_fallback_count for tracking fast-path exit rates.

HostcallRequestQueue (extensions_js.rs): Replaces the simple
VecDeque<HostcallRequest> with a two-tier bounded queue: a fixed-capacity
fast-path ring (256 slots) and a bounded overflow deque (2048 slots).
push_back returns FastPath/OverflowPath/Rejected discriminant for
telemetry. Adds HostcallQueueTelemetry snapshot, drain_all, and
configurable capacities via PiJsRuntimeLimits. HostcallTracker now
records enqueued_at_ms for queue-time measurement.

extension_dispatcher.rs: Stores PolicySnapshot alongside ExtensionPolicy
and routes all capability checks through snapshot.lookup() instead of
policy.evaluate_for().

scheduler.rs: Adds enqueue_hostcall_completions() for batching multiple
hostcall completions in a single scheduler mutation pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
